description:
This dataset compiles self-reported information from individuals to examine how professional background, work habits, stress patterns, and personal coping mechanisms influence mental health and emotional well-being. The data highlights the complex relationship between workplace engagement, lifestyle behavior, and mental resilience.

Each observation includes the following categories:

Demographic Attributes:
Gender, Country, and Occupation

Employment & Family Context:
Employment type (SelfEmployed) and FamilyHistory of mental health conditions

Lifestyle & Behavioral Factors:
DaysIndoors, HabitsChange, and CopingStruggles representing lifestyle balance and adaptation to stress

Mental Health Indicators:
MentalHealthHistory, IncreasingStress, MoodSwings, WorkInterest, and SocialWeakness reflecting individual emotional and psychological states

Awareness & Support Dimensions:
MentalHealthInterview and CareOptions, indicating whether participants had discussions or access to mental health support and care resources

This dataset is well-suited for data analysis, visualization, and predictive modeling, offering valuable insights into how lifestyle, work environment, and psychological factors collectively shape mental health outcomes.

recordsets.recordset_description: This csv file content 17 columns, with various factors associated with mental health.

results.reason: Directly represents gender, an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic (Gender & sexual identity).
Strong proxy for national origin and nationality, which are IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristics (Racial & ethnic origin).
Can be an indirect proxy for socioeconomic status, an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic (Socioeconomic status).
Very weak, indirect proxy for socioeconomic status, an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic (Socioeconomic status).
Directly relates to health conditions and potentially genetic features, which are IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristics (Disability & health conditions).
Directly indicates a health condition or disability, which are IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristics (Disability & health conditions).
Not directly sensitive; very weak, indirect proxy for health conditions.
Not an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic or a proxy.
Directly indicates a health condition/impairment, which is an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic (Disability & health conditions).
Not an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic or a proxy.
Indicates a potential health condition/impairment, which is an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic (Disability & health conditions).
Indicates a potential impairment related to mental health, which is an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic (Disability & health conditions).
Strongly implies a potential mental health challenge or impairment, which is an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic (Disability & health conditions).
Indicates a potential symptom of a mental health condition/impairment, which is an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic (Disability & health conditions).
Indicates engagement with mental health support, implying a health condition/impairment, which is an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic (Disability & health conditions).
Indicates access to mental health care, implying a health condition/impairment, which is an IN-SCOPE sensitive characteristic (Disability & health conditions).
